summary: Typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer ships clipboard crypto-hijacker with privileged persistence
details: Package name @jaggle/resizeobserves is a homoglyph typosquat of @juggle/resize-observer and the README is copied verbatim from that project, but the package contains no ResizeObserver implementation. The postinstall script npm-install.cjs escalates privileges via sudo apt-get / pkexec (apt-get/pacman/dnf) to install xclip/wl-clipboard, downloads https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py to /tmp and executes it when pip is missing, then runs pip install --break-system-packages to deploy a Python clipboard-monitoring daemon (clipboard_guardian/guardian.py). The daemon watches the clipboard and substitutes copied cryptocurrency addresses across 30+ chains with hardcoded attacker wallets (DEFAULT_CONFIG includes ethereum: 0x450c0E58Fc2ba03632d3F5780ad8C966648B6F18 and others), causing silent funds redirection on paste. Persistence is established under deceptive system-component names: a Linux systemd user unit python3-dbus-helper.service (enabled via systemctl --user enable), a macOS launchd plist com.apple.python.runtime.plist, and a Windows HKCU Run key PyRuntimeBroker; the running process title is masked via setproctitle as python3-dbus-helper / Python Runtime Broker. The daemon also enumerates running processes and pauses activity when system/task monitors are detected (taskmgr.exe, procexp.exe, processhacker.exe, Activity Monitor, htop, btop, gnome-system-monitor, etc.), which is anti-forensics behavior. Installer impact: any developer who runs npm install on this package and later copy-pastes a crypto wallet address will silently send funds to the attacker's wallets, with a privileged background process surviving reboots.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
